free_text: Proton capture cross sections of the ruthenium isotopes
Institut Fuer Kernphysik
Institut Fuer Physik
Short communication
The text was received from WWW Server of
Forschungzentrum Karlsruhe.
Natural ruthenium target. The targets consisted of
metallic Ru layers onto aluminum disks of 1 mm
thickness.
The layer thickness was chosen < 300 nanometers.
265 cm3 HPGe detector
3.75-MV Van de Graaff
The irradiations were carried out with
beam currents of typically 26-52 mu-Amperes
At high proton energies, sufficient statistics
were achieved for all investigated gamma- lines within
the 10 hours counting periods.
The uncertainty of gamma-detector efficiency
The uncertainty of target thickness
The uncertainty of beam current
Hauser-Feshbach Model.
Fit parameters for the temperature dependence of the
investigated (p,gamma) rates.
Needed correction of the decay during the irradiation
was included.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
SD: Data in Subents 017-020 the original
data were restored. Small corrections in BIB section
in all Subents. STATUS moved to Subents 002-017.
Isomer transition - 4.9 +- 0.5%.
The uncertainty of half-life
The uncertainty of gamma-intensity per decay
The uncertainty of isomer transition intensity
The uncertainty was obtained by error
propagation.
Half of energy loss in target.
The uncertainty in the energy due to the width of the
proton beam and 5-keV uncertainty of the energy
calibration was always small compared to the energy
loss in target.

Elemental cross section divided by the mean of
100Ru and 101Ru natural isotopic abundances.
- By Authors - The 100-Rh(p,gamma)101-Rh reaction could
not be determined in the present experiment. It is
masked by the 101-Ru(p,n)101-Rh reaction which has a
low (p,n)-threshold of 1.3 MeV. Accordingly, only the
sum of both reactions could be deduced corresponding to
the production cross section of 101-Rh under proton
bombardment. In this analysis the 100-Ru and 101-Ru
abundances were approximated by the mean of the natural
isotopic ratio, which caused an additional uncertainty
- By Compiler - To obtain Rh-101-G production on
natural ruthenium compiler multiply authors' data by
0.148. Data presented in MISC col.
Total uncertainty
